title: Dix Rivan
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]]|[[Darth Rivan]] or [[Dix (disambiguation)|other individuals named Dix]]}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name=Dix Rivan |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], above [[Gall/Legends|Gall]]Dix Rivan is [[Death/Legends|killed]] above [[Gall/Legends|Gall]] during the [[Battle of Gall]], according to the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]], the events of which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. |species= |gender= |pronouns=He/him[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Look out, Dixie!|Wedge Antilles, just before Dix Rivan's death at Gall|}} '''Dix Rivan''', known as '''Dixie''', was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. He flew as [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]] in the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Although Rivan was a shy, introverted type, he considered his fellow Rogue Squadron [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] his family. As the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron's]] rear guard, he was always watching out for his friends in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]], flying to their rescue when needed. Rivan participated in Rogue Squadron's mission to help [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] attempt to rescue the [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]-encased [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] from the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Gall/Legends|Gall]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. As Rogue Squadron engaged [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval forces]] in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] above Gall, Rivan's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] was destroyed by an attacking [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]]. He was the only member of Rogue Squadron [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the [[Battle of Gall|battle]], in which the Rebels failed to free Solo. ==Biography== ===Rogue Squadron's rear guard=== {{Quote|Good to see you again, Luke. I hope you've got something interesting cooked up for us; things have been a little slow lately.|Rogue Leader Wedge Antilles, to Luke Skywalker|}} Dix Rivan was a member of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]],''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' the elite [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] assigned to the roving [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Rebel Alliance High Command headquarters]],[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] in the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], flying under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]]. Because of Rivan's shy personality, no one in Rogue Squadron knew where he came from or why he fought with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], but most assumed his [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and that he joined to get revenge. Rivan served as Rogue Squadron's rear guard, either in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] or on the ground, always watching out for his squadronmates and racing to someone's rescue when needed, although his recklessness put his own life at risk. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], Rogue Squadron established a small temporary [[Rebel base (Kile)|command base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Kile]], [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Zhar/Legends|Zhar]], in the [[Zhar system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Rivan and the Rogues had sneaked into the [[Star system/Legends|system]] for a quick hit-and-fade mission against the [[Cadavine Sector Fleet Imperial Enclave|Imperial Enclave]] on the nearby moon of [[Gall/Legends|Gall]], where the Rebellion had learned the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] was holding the [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]-encased [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in preparation for delivery to the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]. Rivan and the Rogues planned to attack the [[Imperial Enclave]]'s [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval forces]] in orbit over Gall to create a distraction that they hoped would buy enough [[Time/Legends|time]] for a small team—including [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], and the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]], flying his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']]—to sneak in and rescue Solo from Fett. ===Battle of Gall=== {{Quote|Fine shootin', Rogue Leader.''"
"''Thanks, Dix.|Dix Rivan and Luke Skywalker, during the Battle of Gall|}} Upon the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} arrival in the Zhar system, Rivan and Rogue Squadron escorted Organa and Calrissian, along with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], flying in his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]], to the Rebels' moon base on Kile, where the group made their battle preparations. Although Skywalker temporarily joined the Rogues for the rescue mission as the thirteenth squadron member, flying as honorary [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]], they were vastly outnumbered, required to hold off two [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyers]]The two [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] participating in the [[Battle of Gall]], as depicted in [[Shadows of the Empire 2|''Shadows of the Empire'' 2]], can be identified as [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyers]] based on the visual details of their three primary [[Destroyer-I ion engine/Legends|thrust nozzles]]. These [[Engine/Legends|thrust nozzles]] visually correspond to those found aboard the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']] (seen [[:File:WhoWillTicketVaderForLittering-ESB.jpg|here]] and [http://theforce.net/swtc/Pix/Xbrooklyn/Isd31.jpg here]), which is confirmed to be an ''Imperial II''-class by ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', as opposed to those found aboard the Star Destroyer [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']] (seen [http://theforce.net/swtc/Pix/Xbarbican/eydc5/5r22isd.jpg here]), which is confirmed to be an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] by ''[[Vader: The Ultimate Guide]]''. and their combined complement of 144 [[TIE series/Legends|TIE starfighters]]. As the Rogues reached Gall and made their initial approach on the [[Unidentified Imperial II-class Star Destroyer (Gall, dayside)|first Star Destroyer]], Rivan erupted into an excited battle cry over the squadron's [[Comm unit/Legends|comm channel]]. Skywalker considered scolding Rivan to cut the unofficial comm chatter, but he refrained, knowing just how Rivan felt—there was nothing quite like flying into [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]]. [[File:Dix-Gall-SOTEE.png|thumb|right|300px|Dix Rivan's X-wing fighter (far left) is destroyed during the Battle of Gall.]] During the ensuing [[Battle of Gall]], Rivan survived the first few waves of attacking [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]], complimenting Skywalker for his own fine performance in quickly dispatching several of the enemy, but his luck soon ran out. A TIE Fighter maneuvered its way below Rivan's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] and began firing at the exposed underbelly. Rivan tried to evade the attack by turning away to starboard, but he was too late. The X-wing exploded in a [[Fire/Legends|fiery]] blaze, killing Rivan instantly. Rivan was the only Rogue to lose his life during what would prove to be an unsuccessful mission for the Rebellion. Organa and Calrissian failed to rescue Solo from Fett, who escaped the Imperial Enclave on Gall with his [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] in tow. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Yeeehhaaawww!|Dix Rivan flies into combat during the Battle of Gall|}} Dix Rivan, affectionately known to Rogue Squadron as "Dixie," was a quiet individual. He came off as a shy, private person, and as a result nobody in Rogue Squadron knew the details of why he fought with the Rebel Alliance. Most assumed the Empire had killed his parents and so he sought vengeance, but Rivan did not seem like the vengeful type. Instead, Rivan treated the Rogues as his family, and he enjoyed the post-mission camaraderie of his fellow [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]], even if he listened far more than he spoke. In his duty as the squadron's rear guard, Rivan was always there to bail his friends out of trouble during combat, but he did not watch his own back as well as he should, which proved to be his undoing during the mission to Gall. He allowed an enemy TIE to sneak up underneath him and destroy his X-wing during the battle. ==Skills and abilities== Rivan commanded knowledge in [[Language/Legends|languages]], [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] systems, and street survival. He possessed [[Technician/Legends|mechanical ability]] in [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]], [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] operation, X-wing starfighter [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]], starship [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]], and starship [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]; and technical ability in repulsorlift repair. ==Equipment== Rivan's standard Rogue Squadron equipment included a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and his [[Rebel flight suit]]. ==Behind the scenes== The character named "Dix" first appeared in the [[May]] [[1996]] novel [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'']], written by [[Steve Perry]], in which he serves as the only pilot in Rogue Squadron to die during the Rebel Alliance's mission to rescue Han Solo from Boba Fett on Gall. Dix was subsequently provided a last name, "Rivan," and a brief background history in the [[June]] 1996 ''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer|Peter Schweighofer]]. A [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Shadows of the Empire'' minicomic]], originally released in 1996 [[Wikipedia:Ertl Company|Ertl]] ''[[Star Wars]]'' model-kit packages, features a panel depicting the Battle of Gall, in which a single X-wing fighter is shown blowing up.[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Ertl minicomic]] Given that Rivan is the only Rogue to die at Gall in the ''Shadows of the Empire'' novel, this article concludes that the destroyed X-wing in the comic is Rivan's fighter. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] {{1st|in book}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (abridged audiobook)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' abridged audiobook]] *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (junior novelization)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' junior novelization]] *[[Shadows of the Empire 2|''Shadows of the Empire'' 2]] {{Imo}} starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]}} ==Sources== *''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Rivan}} *{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Luke Scores a TIE|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC015.jpg}} {{Imo}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]] |years=[[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]] |before=Eventually [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |before-years=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]According to ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] flew as [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]] during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. |after=Eventually [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]][[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 1|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'' 1]] |after-years=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]According to [[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 1|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'' 1]], [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] served as [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]] as of the [[Standard week/Legends|week]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.}} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dix Rivan |es=Dix Rivan |hu=Dix Rivan |nl=Dix Rivan }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Rivan
